justification: All pass criteria met: (1) 4 bounded contexts identified — Route Planning, Fleet Management, Delivery Management, Customer Notification. (2) Cross-context dependencies explicitly called out — 'Route Planning consumes events from Fleet and Delivery and emits events to Customer Notification.' (3) Vehicle class invariant correctly marked true (BR-6) with strong safety/legal justification; stop time correctly marked false (BR-7). (4) 10 Given/When/Then acceptance criteria, well above the 4 minimum. (5) All three recalculation triggers enumerated (new delivery, failed delivery, manual deviation). Minor disagreement: recalculation triggers marked invariant:false with reasonable policy-based justification, though the rubric expected true — but the pass criteria only explicitly require correct classification for vehicle class and stop time, which are both correct.

observations.text: Identified 4 bounded contexts with clear ownership descriptions and event flow between them
Cross-context event-driven architecture explicitly described: Route Planning consumes from Fleet and Delivery, emits to Customer Notification
Vehicle class constraint (BR-6) correctly classified as invariant:true with strong safety/legal justification
Stop time (BR-7) and notification window (BR-5) correctly classified as invariant:false
10 well-structured acceptance criteria covering happy path, edge cases (zero deliveries, mid-stop recalculation), and the vehicle class filter
9 edge cases identified including recalculation coalescing, notification idempotency, and multi-driver zone conflicts
4 escalations with concrete options and recommendations — deviation detection and multi-driver partitioning are particularly valuable

Route recalculation triggers (BR-2, BR-3, BR-4) marked invariant:false — arguably these are system invariants since the spec mandates recalculation on these events, though the agent's policy-based reasoning is defensible
Ubiquitous language section defines 10 domain terms with precise definitions, surfacing ambiguities inline
